title: Mixer Initialization, Channels, Latency, Volume, Mute, and Cue Alternatives · Behavior Case: Channel Budget
summary: Audio initializes late with wrong parameters, rapid shots cut off critical warnings, one slider controls everything, mute is unavailable, missing audio crashes launch, and gameplay meaning is sound-only. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the channel budget increment in bounded accessible audio and cue system. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.
Configure mixer frequency, format, channels, buffer, and initialization before loading sounds and record platform limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 2443 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. A defensible solution applies Channel Budget, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded accessible audio and cue system.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-audio-mixer-cues-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-audio-mixer-cues-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_audio_mixer_cues_5(critical_events: int = 5, visual_alternatives: int = 5, channels_reserved: int = 3, clipped_sounds: int = 0, mute_available: bool = True, separate_volume: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if critical_events < 0 or channels_reserved < 1:
        return False, critical_events, "audio-domain"
    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:
        return False, clipped_sounds, "channel-starvation"
    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:
        return False, channels_reserved, "audio-control"
    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events
    return True, channels_reserved, "accessible-audio"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

    if visual_alternatives != critical_events:
        return False, visual_alternatives, "cue-alternative"
    if clipped_sounds > 0:

    if not mute_available or not separate_volume:

    assert visual_alternatives == critical_events

options.text: For repair the channel budget defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Channel Budget, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.
Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the channel budget defect evidence boundary.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Channel Budget model · Theory Channel Budget
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Channel Budget: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9633 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Channel Budget model · Theory Channel Budget.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Channel Budget model · Theory Channel Budget, keep Channel Budget connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Channel Budget model · Theory Channel Budget, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Channel Budget precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Channel Budget model · Theory Channel Budget, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 9633 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to own mixer initialization, channel budgets, latency, volume and mute controls, and non-audio alternatives for every critical cue; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Reserve or prioritize channels for critical cues and define overlap, stealing, cooldown, and device-loss behavior.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Channel Budget model · Theory Channel Budget, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Calling Sound.play guarantees the cue will be heard.
